detail description: dummy users with a sudden spike in failed login attempts after a long period of inactivity, which may be an account takeover attempt.
A user will fail this check if they have been inactive for {{numberOfDays}} or more days and experience at least {{dummy}} account probing attempt/s.
recommended action: Investigate the source of failed login attempts and update dummy rules.
Check if the dummy was in any known data breaches.
Follow recommended remediation for dummy Users.

Trigger an access review with the user’s manager to verify that the dummy account still needs access. If the account is unneeded, suspend it. Otherwise, continue monitoring it for dummy and suspend it after a grace period.

detail description: Detects users logging in from an unmanaged device in the last 7 days.
For closer inspection, Identity Intelligence will give an indication of the event, IP address, if the device was managed.
recommended action: We recommend directly alerting the users to recommend using their managed devices. If the behavior persists, consider alerting their manager.

detectionFields.value: AB, Dummy Resources & Diversity, Equity & Inclusion

detail description: Detects dummy users logging in from rare and unusual browsers that are on your blocklist, or not on your allow list.
Dummy browser activity can be a security issue due to the latency of applying patches to those browsers.
It may also indicate that a specific browser differs from what is typically utilized by the dummy end user, which could highlight dummy behavior that should be investigated.

Device prevalence is determined by looking at all successful events over 90 days across all connected integration instances, and automatically excludes any events where the browser, user agent or OS is 'Null' or 'Empty'. The 'Device prevalence threshold' setting uses a percentage of events coming from a particular browser to determine what is or isn't considered rare by the dummy setting.
The dummy check setting, which is enabled by default, will detect rare browsers if the device prevalence is less than 5 times in the last 7 days.
recommended action: Please review the activity of users tracked with a rare browser. Evaluate the user alongside other security anomalies like dummy locations or dummy login failures. You can exclude this user from the check or add the browser to your allowed list.
